programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: This newsmagazine investigates intriguing crime and justice cases that touch on all aspects of the human experience. Over its long run, the show has helped exonerate wrongly convicted people, driven the reopening -- and resolution -- of cold cases, and changed numerous lives. CBS News correspondents offer an in-depth look into each story, with the emphasis on solving the mystery at its heart. The program and its team have earned critical acclaim, including multiple Emmy, Peabody and Edward R. Murrow Awards.

So Whitman hires hardened, cynical Buck Wyatt (Robert Taylor) to be their guide across the inhospitable frontier. But as disaster strikes on the trail, Buck just might discover that these women are stronger than he thinks.

programme.title: The Texan: Dangerous Ground
programme.desc: A rancher warns Bill and the railroad workers not to put track down through the land his cattle graze on, even though it is government land he shoots one of the workers, which sends the rest of the workers into a lynching mood.

programme.title: The Legend of 5 Mile Cave
programme.desc: A boy enchanted with tales from the Old West unlocks a mystery that could save their family farm. While his widowed mother struggles to avoid foreclosure, a mysterious stranger arrives looking for a room to rent. A bond with the boy grows as the drifter recounts breathtaking stories of Shooter Green, a legendary gunslinger accused of Arizona's biggest gold robbery. The truth is revealed, along with the secret of the drifter's past when a crooked lawman appears, hot on the trail of the stolen gold. Past and present collide in this tale of action, adventure and drama. Stars Adam Baldwin (The Last Ship, Full Metal Jacket, The Patriot), Jeremy Sumpter (Peter Pan, Soul Surfer), Jill Wagner (Braven, Christmas Cookies), and Jet Jurgensmeyer (Last Man Standing, American Sniper).

programme.title: County Line
programme.desc: When his best friend, Sheriff Clint Thorne (Jeff Fahey, Texas Rising, Justified), is brutally gunned down, former neighboring county sheriff, Alden Rockwell (Tom Wopat, The Dukes Of Hazzard, Longmire) is left with many questions and no answers. After the investigation stalls and his replacement, Sheriff Preston (Grant Goodeve, Eight is Enough, Dynasty) turns a blind eye, Alden takes the case into his own hands. Uncovering a web of crime, deceit, and corruption that spreads through two counties, Alden is caught in a deadly criminal network with no backup, no badge, and local diner owner, Maddie (Patricia Richardson, Home Improvement, The West Wing) as his only ally. Seeking justice at any cost, Alden must find out who's behind this criminal enterprise, even if some lines must be crossed.

programme.title: Wagon Train
programme.desc: Wagon Train - The Jud Steele Story: A posse pursues Jud Steele, a wanted gunslinger, and his partner. They join the wagon train to rest their horses. Steele dies trying to steal fresh horses. Hawks returns his belongings to the sheriff, who says Steele died five years ago.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Kit Carson: Fearless Fighter: While coming of age on America's frontier, Kit Carson sets a new standard for steely nerves and fearlessness. programme.desc: In this episode the Bean Sportfishing Team joins Captain Dylan Bryan aboard the B n' M for some afternoon Snapper fishing. They target Mangrove Snappers as well as some deep water Mutton Snappers! If you're interested in booking a charter with Captain Dylan on the B n' M, you may contact him directly by calling (305) 393-0848. You can also follow him on Instagram: @captdylanbryan!

programme.title: Bean Sportfishing
programme.sub-title: Bean Sportfishing - "Fishing in Fiji, Part I
programme.desc: In this episode, we journey through the Fijian islands, stopping at Kadavu. We lodge at Oneta Resort and go fishing with Captain Sims, alongside Don & Niu. Our catches range from Coral Trouts and Dogtooth Tunas to Longnose Emperors and the formidable Giant Trevally. Dive into our stunning escapades on Ono Island, a hidden gem in Fiji. Don't miss Part II coming next week!

programme.title: Wild Bearings Outdoors
programme.sub-title: Wild Bearings Outdoors - "Bradley Fork in Great Smoky Mountain National Park in NC
programme.desc: Co-hosts Sam Johnson and Chris Sloan explore and fly-fish the amazing Bradley Fork watershed in GSMNP. Viewers are invited to tag along as they camp on the river and search the waters for the rare and elusive Southern Appalachian Brook Trout. They'll also showcase the watershed's rich history about historic sawmills, Native Americans, early settlers, and the stunning scenery of the destination.

programme.title: Wild Bearings Outdoors
programme.sub-title: Wild Bearings Outdoors - "Soque River Trophy Waters in North GA
programme.desc: In this episode Sam and Chris take country music's “#1 Hit” star Chad Brock fly fishing for the first time on the legendary Soque River in the North Georgia mountains. Viewers will enjoy as they teach Chad how to find and catch trophy trout, and then explore the river's history and amazing scenery. Big “T” Tomasovich provides added insight and guidance finding the trophy trout.

programme.title: Wild Bearings Outdoors
programme.sub-title: Wild Bearings Outdoors - "The Davidson River Watershed in Western NC
programme.desc: In this episode Sam R. Johnson, author of "Fly Fishing the Blue Ridge Parkway" and his Wild Bearings Outdoors co-host Chris Sloan, take you to one of the most famous trout streams in the eastern United States. Trout Unlimited calls it a “A Top 100 Trout Stream in America,” and they will show you why! And to add even more fun, they've invited Kevin Howell of Davidson River Outfitters to come along and chase wild trout in the Davidson's headwaters and some huge trophy trout in Kevin's managed water. It's a firsthand account of the area's history and the fly fishing opportunities along this famous North Carolina river.

programme.title: Wild Bearings Outdoors
programme.sub-title: Wild Bearings Outdoors - "The Watauga River in Eastern TN
programme.desc: Sam R. Johnson, author of "Fly Fishing the Blue Ridge Parkway", and Wild Bearings Outdoors co-host Chris Sloan, provide a firsthand look at the many trout fishing opportunities along the Wilbur Dam tailwater on the Watauga River in eastern TN. They will also fish one of the Watauga's larger tributaries, as well as Beaver Dam Creek close to the VA state line. Lots of fishing action, with details of the river's history, facts about the area's early settlers, Native Americans, local towns, how to get there and where and what to fish for.

programme.title: Wild Bearings Outdoors
programme.sub-title: Wild Bearings Outdoors - "The Etowah River in North GA
programme.desc: Tag along with Sam R. Johnson, author of "Fly Fishing the Blue Ridge Parkway", and Wild Bearings Outdoors co-host Chris Sloan, fish the trophy trout infested waters of the Etowah as it flows through the largest and most scenic winery in North GA. Watch them as they take Montaluce's General Manager, Matthew Garner and teach him how to flyfish and catch trophy trout. Then they'll step out of the river and into the incredibly beautiful Montaluce Winery and its sprawling vineyard. You'll see lots of fishing action, tour the winery and learn about wine making, explore the river's history, as well as that of the Native Americans, settlers, gold miners, and see some beautiful sights. Professional guide Big “T” Tomasovich provides added insight and teaches Matthew how to fly-fish on a 100 year old bamboo rod.

programme.desc: Norman wakes up one morning to find that the whole of Pontypandy is flooded! Fireman Sam is up with Tom Thomas in his helicopter surveying the extent of the flood. The Jones family are stranded on the Wholefish Café roof. // Today the Pontypandy...

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Sam, Elvis and Penny are called to an emergency on the cliff! They arrive in no time following all the correct procedures. The training exercise has been a success. They are practicing for Mike's big rocket launch that is happening that afternoon....

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Sharing the joys and traditions of the holiday season with your little one is truly one of the greatest gifts of parenthood, and Baby Santa's Music Box makes this magical time even more special! Celebrate by treating your baby and yourself to a captivating wonderland of bright lights, sparkling ornaments, sleigh bells, toys, winter scenes, jolly Santas and more -- all accompanied by beloved holiday melodies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby's innate curiosity about all they see and hear increases on a daily basis. Before long, they begin to associate words with objects and to imitate and articulate sounds. Why not give their eyes and ears something wonderful to feast upon? Baby Shakespeare presents little ones with 12 common words that can be found within the rich context of classic poems and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Join our playful puppet host, Bard the Dragon, on a fun-filled adventure using everyday words such as flower, cat, and moon, and beautiful scenery from nature. World Of Words lets you and your little one share the excitement of discovering new words together!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's Favorite Places: First Words Around Town
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby MacDonald: A Day On the Farm
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Every stage is an exciting adventure with your newborn. As babies grow, they absorb a wide variety of sights and sounds. And with so much to see and hear, infants seem to enjoy, and be most soothed by, gentle sounds such as your voice or pleasant, restful music. Treat your child -- and yourself -- to the delights of Baby Bach Musical Adventure, an award-winning program that exposes babies to the joy and majesty of classical music while presenting them with stimulating, colorful images.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Your little one will want to keep a beat, snap his fingers and march his feet! Join new characters Pillie, the musical woodpecker, and Coco, the dancing cockatoo, and share the excitement of rhythm with your baby. World of Rhythm explores rhythm in music, rhythm in nature and rhythm as a form of expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Between 12 and 24 months, little ones become increasingly interested in more complex ideas and begin to explore numbers and counting. Numbers Nursery introduces little ones to numerals 1 through 5 in a captivating, interactive way that invites them to join in, clap their hands and count out loud.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's First Sounds: Discoveries for Little Ears
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies see a little girl and her brother who love salad. They go shopping in a market a market with their grandma to buy some salad to eat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy show us how to make a Turban. The Tubbies go for a walk, but Po, Laa-Laa and Dipsy want to play with their favourite things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa dances with her ball. A little boy tells us why he likes to live in his block of flats. A balloon appears in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies follow a balloon.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A group of children sing some Christmas carols to the Teletubbies. Tinky Winky opens his present to find a beautiful star inside. The Teletubbies follow it all over Teletubbyland, but where will is end up?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa run into Home Hill to say 'Eh-oh', but where 's Po? Some children help their dad to wash his car. They get very wet and soapy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies want to go to sleep, but Tinky Winky finds Po already asleep in his bed. So he goes to look for somewhere else to sleep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and teaches the Teletubbies a winter nursery rhyme. Po's present is a little frosty cloud that begins to snow. All the Teletubbies laugh as they play in the snow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa sit down to eat some Tubby Custard. Po wants some too. She tries to eat the other's Tubby Custard, but they won't let her.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies crawl through the arch in Home Hill and watch Alex Pascall and some children sing 'Handy Hands'.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet appears and the Teletubbies dance, before watching two girls do some Flamenco dancing with their teacher. It's time for the Teletubbies to wash their feet. They love using their Tubby Sponges.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children are playing football in the park. Laa-Laa and Po love playing with the ball. While they play they sing a song about the ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time for the Teletubbies to do their exercises. A voice trumpet rises and the Teletubbies stretch up and down to it's instructions.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and teaches the Teletubbies a nursery rhyme. Then they watch Becky go for a ride on a Tram. Po rides her scooter too fast and knocks the other Tubbies over.

programme.title: Let's Go to the Firehouse
programme.desc: Barney and his friends learn what it's like to be a firefighter!

programme.title: Let's Play School
programme.desc: Baby Bop wishes she could go to school, so Barney makes her dreams a reality.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: Lights! Camera! Action! - A Movie Adventure
programme.desc: Graces food drive isnt going very well until Melanie offers to help. Melanie comes up with the perfect idea to get people to donate.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: To Catch a Thief - A Mystery Adventure
programme.desc: When several sports balls suddenly disappear without a trace, its detective BJ to the rescue as he dons the trench coat and fedora.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: The Magic Lamp - A Travel Adventure
programme.desc: Layla celebrates her birthday in the park with foods that arent traditionally American and doesnt want her friends to think they are weird.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.sub-title: The Amazing Captain Pickles - A Hero Adventure
programme.desc: BJ learns a lesson about bragging when, playing superhero Captain Pickles, he tells everyone that he can leap over the moon!

programme.title: Land of Make Believe
programme.desc: Barney, BJ, Baby Bop and their new friends discover anything can happen in the Land of Make Believe.

programme.title: Let's Make Music
programme.desc: It's time for a musical adventure at Barney's Fantasy Fair! Barney, BJ, and Baby Bop help a little girl named Melanie.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney has a tee-rific surprise, Cousin Riff is moving to town! When they cant decide what to do for the day, they decide to put on a puppet show.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Wendy's Magic Birthday / Bob's Egg And Spoon Race
programme.desc: It is Wendy's birthday, but she doesn't want any fuss. / The town is practicing for an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Tea Set Travis / Wendy's Big Match
programme.desc: Bob and the machines are picking up rubbish. / Bob's entered the 'Brightest Builders' Yard Competition'.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.desc: Bob is refurbishing the bandstand in the park. / Farmer Pickles asks Bob to build a run for Scruffty.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: While refurbishing Farmer Pickles' cottage Bob finds a hedgehog. / Bob builds a shed in Farmer Pickles' field.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Roley & The Rockstar / Scruffty The Detective
programme.desc: Bob is installing a garden pond for rock star Lennie Lazenby. / The museum curator is planning a Time Capsule.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in a local park. / A burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini's needs fixing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Beasley is having a conservatory built. / Bob and Wendy have been invited to a lunch at the Town Hall.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Spud's Statue / Pilchard and the Field Mice
programme.desc: Bob is building a pavilion in the park. / Mr Ellis needs Bob to replace an old rotten beam in his cottage.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Beasley's Noisy Pipes / Lofty's Jungle Fun
programme.desc: Mr Beasley's pipes are making banging noises./ Bob and the gang build a pedestrian crossing.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Mr Bentley - Dogsitter / Travis Gets Lucky
programme.desc: Bob and the gang convert a house into two separate flats. / Farmer Pickles is having problems with mice.

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.title: Bob the Builder

programme.desc: Mr Bentley is organising a winter fair. / Bob installs an air conditioning unit at Mr Sabatini's restaurant.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team build a special eco-home. / The team are building Sunflower Valley's first geodesic dome.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Bob and the Goalie / Dizzy Goes Camping
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park. Farmer Pickles has decided to open a camp site on his land.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.sub-title: Lofty To The Rescue / Dizzy's Bird Watch
programme.desc: Bob, Dizzy and Roley repair the pavement in the town centre. / Bob and Lofty make an exciting discovery.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Berry Best You Can Bee
programme.desc: Strawberry is at Orange's store to mail a birthday card to a friend in Big Bitty City. Just after she drops the card into the mail slot, an ill postal carrier bee sneezes sending envelopes and post cards flying in all directions. The two girls help Jadeybug, a postal clerk, and Postmaster Bee gather up the strewn letters and the ill bee gets sent home from work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While rehearsing a song alone in her cafe, Strawberry is interrupted by the arrival of Jadeybug. The poor ladybug is stressed out because her twin cousins, Kadiebug and Sadiebug, left her a message that they are coming to visit for a whole week. Jadey is unable to host them because she's having her guest room repainted.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While at Orange's general store to pick up a package, Berrykin Bloom chats with Orange about the pogostick he gave her to help her reach the upper shelves. She is very appreciative. He ends up getting a package of special berry seeds he ordered, not the vacation magazine Orange thought he came for. He explains how the seeds were a better purchase because of all the benefits to the Berryworks but now he can no longer afford a vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Plum decides to hold a dance recital at Strawberry's Café Cabaret stage and sends out a stack of invitations. She forgets, however, to check the sign-up sheet at the Café and schedules it for the same night as Blueberry is scheduled to read to the baby Berrykins. Strawberry tells Plum she cannot hold the recital in the Café on her chosen day because the Café is booked.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is excitedly making their way through the berry batch in search of the elusive Wanderberry. It is to be the dessert at the BerryFest Feast. Everyone is milling about searching for it on their own when Blueberry points out that the rules say the BerryFest Princess is in charge of the search. The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Nothing To Fear But Berries Themselves
programme.desc: Overhearing Blueberry reading a story to the baby Berrykins about the scary Galumphalot, Orange is uncharacteristically shaken. She determines she doesn't want anything scary around her, just relaxation. But while the girls are having a hair-styling party at Lemon's salon, a malfunction at the BerryWorks causes the whole town to go dark. And Orange begins to get really anxious.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Berry Best You Can Bee
programme.desc: Strawberry is at Orange's store to mail a birthday card to a friend in Big Bitty City. Just after she drops the card into the mail slot, an ill postal carrier bee sneezes sending envelopes and post cards flying in all directions. The two girls help Jadeybug, a postal clerk, and Postmaster Bee gather up the strewn letters and the ill bee gets sent home from work.

The poor ladybug is stressed out because her twin cousins, Kadiebug and Sadiebug, left her a message that they are coming to visit for a whole week. Jadey is unable to host them because she's having her guest room repainted.

programme.desc: While at Orange's general store to pick up a package, Berrykin Bloom chats with Orange about the pogostick he gave her to help her reach the upper shelves. She is very appreciative. He ends up getting a package of special berry seeds he ordered, not the vacation magazine Orange thought he came for. He explains how the seeds were a better purchase because of all the benefits to the Berryworks but now he can no longer afford a vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is gathered around to watch the last snowberrykin melt. As the last bit of snow forms a puddle, a cheer goes up, spring is here! It's now time for the annual BerryFest. But with Princess Berrykin out of town on business, who will be the BerryFest Princess and preside over the festival?! Everyone is abuzz with how to go about choosing the Princess and what her duties will be. Postmaster Bee declares the only fair way to go about it is to hold an election. Everyone agrees.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is excitedly making their way through the berry batch in search of the elusive Wanderberry. It is to be the dessert at the BerryFest Feast. Everyone is milling about searching for it on their own when Blueberry points out that the rules say the BerryFest Princess is in charge of the search. The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Doggie Dance No-Show
programme.desc: When the girls discover that Apple's groove booties can teach the pups to dance, Plum gets an idea for a Doggie Dance Show. Wanting to impress them, Huck offers to have TomTom join the show... despite the pup's obvious dislike of performing in public. It's up to Strawberry to help Huck see that he should not force TomTom into something he just doesn't want to do.

programme.title: Foods that Grow
programme.desc: Ready Set Grow! With a garden's worth of greenery, Avery grills up a batch of veggie burgers. Lily heads into the field to unravela mystery: how do greenhouses help veggies grow in winter (Psst. The secret is right under your feet). Then our duo discovers a funny thing about tomatoes – they're actually fruit! And avocados are actually berries! Turn them into dip and discover a delicious treat.

programme.title: Foods that Stick
programme.desc: Lily's coming unglued! Can Matt help her find the fix for chicken fingers that stick to the pan? Avery tackles the art of making Japanese sticky rice at a sushi restaurant. But, when the octopus sushi gives him the willies, will he be able to stick to the task? Our hosts really bond when they compete to see who can make the stickiest snacks. With all this fun, you'll want to stick around!

programme.title: Grandma's Glasses
programme.desc: Sid explores his sense of vision, and discovers that even though Grandma's glasses make things look blurry to him, they make things look clear to her.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Foods that Smell
programme.desc: Lily and Avery follow their noses to find out what gives foods their amazing aromas. Avery enjoys a super garlicky Caesar salad, and then tries to sniff out a cure for his ghastly garlic breath. Lily makes a splash at the fish market where she gets to the bottom of seafood's suspicious smells. Together, they take on the ultimate olfactory challenge: recreating Matt's Amazing Seafood Chowder, by sense of smell alone!

programme.title: Foods that Melt
programme.desc: Watch foods melt before your eyes! Chill out as Lily tries to solve the mystery behind Baked Alaska, a frozen dessert that doesn't melt in the oven. Onthe hunt for a simple snack, Avery visits a local cheese shop and discovers there's moreto cheese than just cheddar! Back at the kitchen with Matt, Lily and Avery use a varietyof cheeses and fresh veggiesto see who can make thebest twist on an ooey-gooey melted favourite: grilled cheese sandwiches!

programme.title: The Sticker Chart
programme.desc: Sid wonders, who needs charts anyway? After a day of investigating, Sid learns that charts are great science tools that help you compare and contrast all kinds of cool stuff like snacks, pets and even chores!

programme.title: The Rolie Polie
programme.desc: At school, he discovers an amazing science tool: the magnifying glass! Sid and his friends explore their world using the magnifying glass to make things look bigger, like freckles on their skin, little pebbles and rolie polie legs!

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Tarzan and Jane
programme.desc: After returning to London, Tarzan and Jane encounter Jane's mother, Angela, a celebrity-cum-journalist who is researching an expose claiming Greystoke Industries harms the environment. The truth is someone is trying to destroy Greystoke Industries. Tarzan and Jane must figure out who this mystery saboteur is while battling fires, snakes, and sneak attacks. With this Mastermind's henchmen trying to stop them at every turn, our young heroes must take part in a thrilling, multi-country adventure in order to save Greystoke Industries and finally put an end to the criminal enterprise that threatens to put the environment and countless lives - human and animal alike - in danger!

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Going Golfing // Case of the Stolen Spectacles
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Up in the Air // AdDRESSing the Problem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Wakey Wakey // Rules of the Road
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: A Colorful Way to Cool Off // Seeing in Believing
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that Rainbow Village friends were playing around the Rainbow fountain to cool off from the heat but water suddenly stopped coming out of the fountain. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries. Kiki accidentally picked magical Plumen berries instead of the delicious rainbow berries she was trying to gather.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Bad Hair Day // Ice is Nice
programme.desc: Ellie's and Jessy's hair are a complete mess after Felicia accidentally causes some Rainbow fruit to fall on them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a hairdresser. // It's winter in Rainbow Village. Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that last night a huge ice mountain appeared so everyone is enjoying an ice sculpture festival. But Jessy seems to have a toothache.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: The ground is all wet in Rainbow Village. What's going on? The friends tell Ruby that Jessy's tears have soaked the ground. Jessy accidentally ate Ellie's magic beans and became as huge as a giant. // Thunderbell is very sad because his best friend, Mr. Sloth, is angry at him. Rainbow Ruby thinks up the best way to apologize to Mr. Sloth, who loves performances. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a choreographer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: How Does Your Garden Grow // Mystery Gifts
programme.desc: Kiki wanted to make a nice garden so she asked the Poppies to do some gardening but they planted the seeds everywhere in the garden, making an absolute mess. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a gardener.// Ling Ling is setting up a 'Thankfulness' tree at the center of the town square. He tells Rainbow Ruby that he is going to hold an event where the village friends can write notes to people that they are thankful to and hang them up on the tree.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are on a train and they are concerned that the train won't stop. The engine is even smoking! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a firefighter. // Paige is extremely upset. It turns out that she's been preparing an awesome fashion show but now it had to be cancelled because Gina accidentally spilled a bunch of fruit onto the stage.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: The Rainbow Wave // The Best Pet You Never Met
programme.desc: Rainbow Village friends are gathered together at the lake to surf on the Rainbow wave that only comes once a year and they need some help. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a lifeguard and helps her friends safely enjoy their surfing. // Thunderbell is looking extremely worried. It turns out that his pet fish has gone missing. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a detective and finds out Thunderbell's pet fish's whereabouts.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: A welcome party for Prince Frederick is taking place in Rainbow Village, but the guest of honour isn't having any fun at all. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a party planner. // The village is a total mess from the paint that spilled from up above. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a clean-up engineer and starts cleaning with her friends.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Dolphin Dilemma // Ruby and the Beast
programme.desc: A Flower Festival is underway in Rainbow Village and Ling Ling has called Rainbow Ruby to help her judge the "best flower" contest. But just as Rainbow Ruby starts looking around at the flowers, Kiki's pet dog Pom Pom starts acting unusual and running around frantically, destroying the festival! // Gina and Jessy are with a huge fish that's injured by the lake. Rainbow Ruby becomes a Marine Biologist. Rainbow Ruby uses her encyclopedia to find out that the fish is actually a dolphin.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Hat Trick // Dancing on the Ice
programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. Rainbow Ruby designs hats where the birds can comfortably sit without disturbing the villagers. // The village friends were planning to have some fun with the rainbow fish but they're all disappointed to discover that the lake has frozen over.

programme.title: Jelly Jamm

Back safe, Miss Dottie takes the stage, this time with her new pal Rumble! // Rev is determined to master Rumble's new wrecking-ball attachment, but things quickly get out of hand when the duo refuse to ask for help. Now they'll need all the help they can get to stop the wayward ball from wrecking all of Fender Bend!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Pond Pirates // The Coolest Girl I Know
programme.desc: When Rev's dad tells about him about treasure sunken in the pond, Rev and Avery set out on a pirate adventure! They outfit Rumble and Alley with new dive equipment to salvage the long-lost toys and cherished memories found within the chest. // When Rev's cousin Katie comes to visit, Avery's jealousy gets the best of her-Avery always thought she was the coolest girl Rev knew! Intent on outdoing Katie, Avery pulls a stunt that she comes to regret.

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Tuff McTruck Trail // Lori's Talent // The Camping Trip // Like Father Like Son
programme.desc: When Rev and Avery enter a famous endurance race, they end up faced with a rescue mission to save their idol himself, the toughest truck around! // When the Fender Bend Quarry needs help, Rev tries to convince Lori and Tipper to share their...

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Fender Bend Friend Day // The Rev & Rumble Fan Club
programme.desc: Rev wants to surprise his friends with a movie celebrating their friendship, so he secretly gathers footage to show they're the best friends ever. When Avery realizes what he's up to, she adds her own clips to the movie - surprising Rev as well! // Owen hosts a virtual review of the gang's adventures with Rev and Rumble, plus all of their awesome gear! He walks his viewers through some of their most memorable moments, from mega stunts to monsters to rescue missions.

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.desc: After many years of working hard on Accelerator Acres, Tilly the tractor is ready to retire from ranch life. Rev wants to find her the perfect new job, but his ideas are too intense for old Tilly. He'll have to adjust to her slower pace first. // Rev and the gang compete for the title of Best Prankster-except Lori sits out, claiming pranks aren't her thing. After a full day of pranks and laughs, the kids gather for the big party that Lori planned for them...but all is not as it seems!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Anything You Can Do // A Very Avery Day
programme.desc: As Rev and Avery compete to see who can be the best helper, they accidentally work against each other and end up making things more complicated for those they // Rev thinks Avery is moving away! Upset, he leads an all-out effort to convince her to stay, showing her that Fender Bend is the best place to live-despite Avery's protests that she's too busy. Finally, Avery confronts their strange behaviour.

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: The Green Phantom // Over Easy Does It
programme.desc: Rev is sure he has the perfect plan to catch an elusive frog, but Avery isn't so sure. After all of his attempts fall through, he's finally ready to accept help-and Avery's idea just might work! Will they finally catch the Green Phantom? // Rev and Avery compete to transport supplies to town for a big event. Avery is faster, so Rev tries to take a short cut to get ahead-but an obstacle stops him in his tracks. How will he finish his delivery in time, let alone win the competition?

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.desc: Rev and Avery go on an adventure to discover the Crystal Cavern. As they enter the first cave, a swarm of bats scare Rumble so much, he takes off through a maze of tunnels! They can't worry about the Crystal Cavern-they have to find Rumble first! // When Molly tells a campfire story about the Fender Bend Beast, the kids' imaginations run wild. Covered in goop and debris, Rumble scares them into thinking he's the Beast! Avery hatches a plan to trap the "Beast" and save Rumble, who's gone missing

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Winter Bestival // The Mystery of Missing Trees
programme.desc: After a snowstorm, Rev and Avery compete for title of Champion of the Winter Bestival! Avery is determined to hold her title from last year. Rev's Dad suggests a "slow and steady" approach to win, but Rev can't resist using "Rumble power!" // When Rumble wakes Rev in the middle of the night, they hear a strange sound, and see lights in the forest. The next day, Ben encourages them to investigate further. Rev and his friends follow the trail of missing trees to discover the truth!

programme.title: Rev and Roll

Back safe, Miss Dottie takes the stage, this time with her new pal Rumble! // Rev is determined to master Rumble's new wrecking-ball attachment, but things quickly get out of hand when the duo refuse to ask for help. Now they'll need all the help they can get to stop the wayward ball from wrecking all of Fender Bend!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Pond Pirates // The Coolest Girl I Know
programme.desc: When Rev's dad tells about him about treasure sunken in the pond, Rev and Avery set out on a pirate adventure! They outfit Rumble and Alley with new dive equipment to salvage the long-lost toys and cherished memories found within the chest. // When Rev's cousin Katie comes to visit, Avery's jealousy gets the best of her-Avery always thought she was the coolest girl Rev knew! Intent on outdoing Katie, Avery pulls a stunt that she comes to regret.

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.desc: Rev and the gang are painting a mural for Four-Wheeled Friendship Day! But with Avery in charge, the friends start to lose confidence in their work. She may be a master when it comes to art, but Avery has a lot to learn about being project leader! // When the pals befriend a mouse in the barn, Rumble can't resist breaking the rules by bringing the little guy a snack. Before long there are mice everywhere! Rev and Rumble will need to shoo away their tiny guests, before Mom and Dad find them!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.desc: When Rev finds out that Bo wants to be a Police Kid, he tells a white lie to make him one for a day! Officer Bo takes the new job too far, ticketing everyone-for everything. Rev will have to tell the truth to fix this mess and get his friend back! // At Camp Sleepaway, Bo is homesick. His friends try to lift his spirits, but after a stinky trip to the outhouse, he's had enough! That is...until a suggestion from Rev helps Bo to see the beauty all around. Maybe camp isn't so bad after all!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Gear Up Garage Band // First Aid Fun
programme.desc: At band practice, Rumble struggles to control his nervous backfiring, even damaging the instruments by accident! Determined not to give up on the festival, the gang improvises and uses makeshift instruments. They even feature Rumble's nervous twitch! // When Rev and Owen begrudgingly agree to join Bo's First Aid Games, they do everything BUT take the lessons seriously. It isn't long before the boys find out that first aid isn't all fun and games, but it IS totally awesome!

programme.title: Rev and Roll
programme.sub-title: Bo to the Rescue // Ice Cream Team
programme.desc: When Bo messes up on a few rescue missions, he decides to quit Bo's Rescue Hotline. Wanting to help their friend, the gang creates fake emergencies for Bo to solve. But as the friends find out, it takes more than trickery to build self-confidence! // As a heatwave hits Fender Bend, Rev and his friends look forward to Ice Cream Dream Day! But when the ice cream truck breaks down, it's up to the gang to find a way to deliver the tasty treats before they all melt.

programme.title: TurboZaurs
programme.desc: The adventures of dinosaur's squad transforming into big machines who are willing to solve any difficulties that kids may face in their everyday life.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Being an ‘only lamb in the flock' Timmy has been used to getting his own way. Now for the first time Timmy is going to have to learn to get along with other little animals. Timmy is going to Nursery! Self-willed and naughty Timmy is going to have to learn how to share things with other youngsters and think about their feelings – not just his. Timmy is a gifted little footballer who needs to learn that it's not enough to kick the ball – you have to pass it too. Harriet Heron and Osbourne Owl, who run the Nursery are going to have their hands full, but Timmy gets it right in the end with a little help from his friends of the furry, feathery and spikey type.

programme.title: Ping and Friends
programme.desc: Follow Ping and her friends as they find musical inspiration in unlikely places!

programme.title: Back to Back
programme.desc: Embark on an electrifying journey as they boldly leap from one heart-pounding expedition to the next, fueled by their unbreakable bond and insatiable thirst for excitement. Get ready to be swept away in a whirlwind of adrenaline-pumping quests, where danger and discovery await them at every turn.

programme.title: Counting With Paula
programme.desc: The show features Paula and her friends, Tim, Billy, Chalky, Calc, and the Numbers as they go on exciting adventures in colorful and exotic places. From the Amazon jungles to the deepest oceans, and to the hottest deserts, Paula and friends solve problems, have fun and make more friends along the way!

programme.title: Helper Car
programme.desc: Cartoon for kids about helper cars: a fire truck, an ambulance, a police car and a tow truck. Why these cars and trucks are useful? Because they help people and other cars: fire truck can put out fire, ambulance helps people, when they are hurt or sick, it could drive them to the hospital. Police car patrols the city. Tow truck help other cars, when they are broken down. Watch new car cartoon and learn about helper cars!

programme.title: Little Malabar
programme.desc: Little Malabar is very curious and very lucky. He gets to travel into the universe as if it were a big playground, and talk to planets and stars to understand where we come from and how our world took form.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, so does their awareness of the world around them. In this season, discover and learn about animals, starting with the ones that your children may encounter at home, to farm animals, all the way to the most unique of creatures from all 7 continents!

programme.title: Everything's Rosie
programme.desc: Join Rosie and her friends as they go on fun adventures, play games and solve problems. Each episode contains an educational message so that kids are engaged while learning. With a focus on friendship the show also promotes positivity through teamwork.

programme.title: Stella And Sam
programme.desc: As they explore the world in all its seasons, Stella and Sam have wild adventures and comical mishaps. They make friends and discover many things - especially about themselves. If Sam is bold enough to rescue Fred from the sprinkler, then maybe he really can be brave enough to have a sleepover in the backyard tent. Every day is a new adventure, and Stella and Sam share the joy of discovering that they have what it takes to face the biggest adventure of all... growing up.

programme.title: Non Stop Hits

programme.title: Oddbods
programme.desc: Seven unusual characters laugh, fool and trip their way through life.

programme.title: My Magic Pet Morphle
programme.desc: Little Mila turns playtime into fun and educational adventures with her magical pet Morphle, who transforms into anything she can dream up.

programme.title: Gecko's Garage
programme.desc: Learning with Gecko and the mechanicals at Gecko's Garage.

programme.title: Franklin and Friends
programme.desc: "Franklin and Friends," based on the children's book series featuring Franklin, is a computer-animated series geared toward preschoolers that aims to teach the kids lessons. The series follows Franklin the turtle, now older than in the books and previous TV series, as he experiences adventures and achieves milestones in the community of Woodland, learning lessons along the way. Franklin's friends referenced in the title include best friend Bear, problem-solving Snail, practical joker Rabbit, natural leader Beaver, inventive Fox and empathetic Goose. The series tackles issues that include personal empowerment, socialization, self-esteem, appreciation of nature and school issues.
